title: QA Conversation A-RAG Cert
description: Certifies the conversation graph store at _forensics/qa_retrieval.sqlite is a QA-native A-RAG datastore instance per QA_ARAG_INTERFACE_CERT.v1, with cross-source messages mapped to canonical (b, e) tuples via Candidate F (gematria + role_rank) grounded in Aiq Bekar [cert 202].

view_substrates.KEYWORD_VIEW: SQLite FTS5 (unicode61) over messages_fts, filtered by orbit sector
view_substrates.SEMANTIC_VIEW: Personalized PageRank (alpha=0.5) over parent/cite/succ/ref edges, scoped by sector

thought_promotion_rules.chatgpt: native content_type in {thoughts, reasoning_recap} triggers role=thought regardless of native_role
thought_promotion_rules.claude: native content[].type == 'thinking' triggers role=thought; thinking strings concatenated into raw_text
thought_promotion_rules.gemini: native chunk.isThought == true triggers role=thought; parts[].text concatenated into raw_text

a2_compliance.rationale: Per 2026-04-09 rule: elements use d=b+e, a=b+2e RAW. Mod reduction is T-operator only.

t2_firewall.notes: Text enters at ingest (observer -> discrete). QA layer operates on integer (b,e,d,a). Scores at FTS/PPR are output-only.

axiom_compliance.A1: b in {1..9}, e in {1..5}, sector labels in {1..9} via dr form
axiom_compliance.A2: d and a derived on read, never stored
axiom_compliance.S1: no ** operator in storage schema or ingest code
axiom_compliance.S2: all tuple columns INTEGER, no float state
axiom_compliance.T1: path time not applicable to storage layer
axiom_compliance.T2: observer-layer scores never feed back into QA state
